error.signature: java.nio.charset.MalformedInputException: Input length = 1 — non-ASCII path or filename

verdict.summary: Java Files.readAllLines() or similar API throws MalformedInputException when file path or content contains Korean/CJK and no charset is specified.

dead_ends.action: Wrap in try-catch and skip the file
dead_ends.why_fails: Silently skipping files with Korean names hides real data; does not fix the encoding issue

dead_ends.action: Convert filename to ASCII with Normalizer
dead_ends.why_fails: Normalizer handles decomposition, not transliteration; Korean chars have no ASCII equivalent

workarounds.action: Explicitly specify StandardCharsets.UTF_8 in all file read/write operations
workarounds.how: Files.readAllLines(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)  // never rely on default charset

workarounds.action: Set -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 JVM argument and use InputStreamReader with explicit charset
workarounds.how: java -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -jar app.jar  // also use new InputStreamReader(is, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
